warning Input Injection Blocked: Your phone is rejecting keyboard and touch input simulations. For Touch ID unlocking and remote controls to work:
  1. Go to Settings > Additional Settings > Developer Options.
  2. Toggle ON "USB debugging (Security settings)" (requires SIM card / Mi login).
  3. Reboot your phone to apply changes, then retry mirroring.

lightbulb Wireless Connection Quick Guide

1

Enable Wireless Debugging

Go to settings > Developer options > Toggle "Wireless Debugging" ON.

2

Scan Network (mDNS)

Click "Scan Network" below to auto-discover your phone IP and connection port.

3

First-Time Pairing

Tap "Pair device with pairing code". Keep it open, enter its Pairing Port & Code on the right, and click "Pair Wireless Device".

4

Connect

Once paired, enter the connection port from the phone's main Wireless Debugging screen, and click "Connect Device".

wifi Wireless Auto-Discovery (mDNS)

bolt Manual Wireless Connect

key Wireless Debugging Pairing

build Diagnostics Toolkit

Mirroring & Camera Modes

Stream your phone screen, sensor camera, or record session natively

desktop_windows

Standard Screen Mirroring

Interact with your phone screen directly on your Mac. Seamless audio streaming output.

PRO
camera

Live Camera Feed

Stream front or back camera directly to your Mac. Supports mic merging and live controls.

volume_up

Audio-Only Mirroring

Listen directly to the Android microphone or system audio stream without rendering video.

videocam

Mirror & Record Session

Launches standard screen mirror and records the entire session as an MP4 directly to your Mac Desktop.

content_paste Universal Clipboard

sync_disabled Status: Inactive

Copy on Phone -> Paste on Mac (Runs automatically in background).


Mac ➔ Phone (Type directly)

Because Android blocks background clipboard writing, click this to instantly type your Mac's clipboard into your phone's active text box.


settings Mirroring Preferences

Customize high-speed parameters and save them locally.

menu_book Documentation & Help

User guide and troubleshooting for ConnectPhone

wifi Connection Setup Guide

ConnectPhone supports both high-speed USB and seamless Wireless connections. For the best experience, we recommend pairing wirelessly over a 5GHz network.

Prerequisites

1. Go to your phone's Settings > About Phone and tap "Build Number" 7 times to unlock Developer Options.

2. Navigate to Settings > System > Developer Options.

3. Enable USB Debugging and Wireless Debugging.

4. Important for Mac control: You must also enable "USB debugging (Security settings)" to allow macOS to simulate screen taps and keystrokes.

Wireless Pairing (First Time)

1. Ensure your Mac and phone are on the exact same Wi-Fi network.

2. Tap directly on the text "Wireless Debugging" on your phone to open its menu.

3. Tap "Pair device with pairing code". A pop-up will show an IP address, Port, and a 6-digit Wi-Fi pairing code.

4. In ConnectPhone's Connection Center, type these exact details into the Pairing section and click Pair.

Connecting (Subsequent Uses)

Once paired, you never need to pair again. Simply ensure Wireless Debugging is turned on, go to the Connection Center, and click Scan Network to magically find and connect your phone!

desktop_windows Screen Mirroring & Audio

Control your phone with zero latency right from your Mac using our built-in mirroring engine powered by scrcpy.

Controls & Hotkeys

• Right Click: Acts as the "Back" button.

• Middle Click: Acts as the "Home" button.

• Scroll Wheel: Scrolls lists and pages natively.

• Cmd + O: Turns your phone's physical screen off while keeping the mirror active (saves battery!).

Audio Routing

By default, Android 11+ routes device audio directly into your Mac. You can disable this or change the target audio output device (like AirPods) from the Preferences tab before starting a mirror.

sync Universal Clipboard & Media

Pulling Screenshots

In the Media & Sync tab, click Fetch Latest. ConnectPhone will scan your phone's standard screenshot directories (DCIM/Screenshots and Pictures/Screenshots). Clicking "Pull" will instantly download the image directly to your Mac's Desktop!

Background Clipboard Sync

Clicking "Start Background Sync" creates a headless, invisible bridge between your devices.

Phone to Mac: Instantly copy text on your Android and press Cmd + V on your Mac.

Mac to Phone Workaround: Since Android 10, Google severely restricts background apps from altering the clipboard for security reasons. To get text from your Mac to your phone without opening a mirror window, we provided the "Type Mac Clipboard on Phone" button. Simply tap a text field on your phone and click the button to inject your Mac's clipboard as secure keystrokes.

fingerprint macOS Touch ID Integration

The Biometric Daemon is an exclusive ConnectPhone feature that bridges Apple's Secure Enclave with Android's lockscreen.

How it Works

If you have this preference enabled, the dashboard continuously monitors your phone's screen state. The moment you wake up your phone to the lockscreen, or encounter an App Lock (like WhatsApp or Banking apps requiring a PIN), your Mac will instantly prompt you for Touch ID. Upon successful authentication, it decrypts and securely injects your Android PIN to unlock it instantly.

Setup & Troubleshooting

Make sure you have saved your correct Android PIN in the Preferences tab. If the daemon stops responding, simply turn your phone screen off and on again to re-trigger the wake event.
